title: Cyperus × deamii
intro: Cyperus × deamii, also known as Deam's flatsedge, is a rare sedge species native to North America, characterized by its distinctive flat stems and small, yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Deam's flatsedge include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Deam's flatsedge is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional folk remedies and modern supplements.
taxonomy: Cyperus × deamii is classified as a hybrid species within the genus Cyperus, which belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This family is commonly known as the sedge family and is a large group of monocots. The Cyperus × deamii is a type of sedge, which is a type of perennial grass-like plant.
